steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The decisive clue is Mrs. Higgins’ direct command to Henry: “you mustn’t stay.” This is not a subtle implication but a clear instruction, indicating the central purpose of the exchange.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice B directly aligns with the command to leave, which is the explicit focus of Mrs. Higgins’ dialogue. The other choices fail for specific reasons:
  • Choice A — 'describe what Henry’s mother does when she goes out with her friends': the text never depicts her outings with friends; it describes her at home telling Henry to leave. ✗
  • Choice C — 'present a detailed account of what Henry’s home looks like': no description of the home’s appearance appears in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'explain why Henry often visits his mother': the text gives no frequency or reason for visits; it only establishes that Mrs. Higgins wants him to leave before her friends arrive. ✗
Mrs. Higgins explicitly tells Henry “you mustn’t stay,” so B is correct — B is correct.
